sections.paragraph:
const. אֹוצַר; pl. אֹוצָרֹות m. (root אָצַר).

(1) [“properly, what is laid up, a store, stock,”] treasure, store, as of corn, food, provision (magazine), 2Ch 11:11 1Ch 27:27 especially of gold, silver, and other precious things, hence used of the treasury of the temple, 1Ki 7:51 of the king, 1 K. 14:26 15:18 בֵּית אֹוצָר “a treasury,” Neh 10:39.

(2) i.q. בּ״ אֹוצָר a storehouse, Joe 1:17 a treasury, 2Ch 32:27.
